video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Philadelphia with Guy Fieri 🔔 | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Standard Tap
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in the Northern Liberties neighborhood of Philadelphia, about six blocks from Center City, to check out a joint. Now get this, they were the first to be serving only locally made drafts in Philadelphia, and maybe one of the first in the country. You think their beer program's impressive? Check out their menu. This is Standard Tap.

dishes.description: Molded chicken pie with roasted chicken, cremini mushrooms, garlic, sage, thyme, parsley, mirepoix, and chicken stock, thickened with a roux. Served with a salad and mustard vinaigrette.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, it's dreamy and creamy. ... If you like chicken pot pie and you want more of the crunchy crust that you have on top, this is what you want. I'll kill her. ... It's, like, super savory. Tons of chicken. It's like a chicken soup with, like, a great pastry puff outside of it. A lot of depth of flavor. This is chicken pie the way it should be.

restaurants.name: Honey's Sit and Eat
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Northern Liberties, Philadelphia, just north of Old City. You know where they signed the Declaration of Independence? To check out a joint that's in a building that some people say was once a produce market. Some say a coffin shop. Some say a bank. Some say even a mattress factory. Now, regardless of what it was then, now it's home to a funky little joint that's serving up a blend of, get this, Jewish food and Southern comfort food. This is Honey's Sit and Eat.

dishes.description: Shredded boiled chicken in a guajillo pepper sauce, combined with a chili base made from nine vegetables and spices, served over Fritos and topped with yellow cheddar cheese and scallions.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. I get the guajillo right off the bat. Right when I got a bite of the chicken. It's a real fresh chili. Great texture. Great flavor. Great crunch. I can really taste the Frito. Mmm. It's the guajillo chicken, dude. It really takes it someplace else. That's not your everyday chili right there, folks.

restaurants.name: The Dining Car
restaurants.guy_intro: In the world of 24-7, when the sun's coming up, it means the end of a late night or the beginning of an early morning. And either way, you're gonna need some grub. The folks here at the Dining Car in Philadelphia well, they've got you covered. And since I was here in 2007, not much has changed. It's still run by the same family with the same chef behind the line. And it's still the talk of the town. This is the Dining Car.

dishes.description: Crepes made with eggs, flour, and clarified butter, filled with sliced ham, natural cure cheddar cheese, and fresh cooked spinach, topped with a supreme sauce and mushrooms.
dishes.guy_response: That's delicious. Crepes Florentine. I haven't tasted anything that good since I was in Paris having crepes.

restaurants.name: Mom Mom's Kitchen
restaurants.guy_intro: So we're here in Bridesburg, right in the northeast side of Philadelphia, to check out a joint where they're using grandma's recipes to make it the real deal. This is Mom Mom's Kitchen.

dishes.description: Pierogi dough made with sour cream, butter, eggs, and flour, filled with thinly sliced ribeye and Cooper Sharp American cheese. Boiled, then seared on a flat-top grill and served with black pepper ketchup, scrambled eggs, caramelized onions, and scallions.
dishes.guy_response: Yeah, that's what I'm thinking. ... That pierogi dough with the sour cream and the whole barnyard of chickens, it's silky-looking. It's luxurious. Melts in your mouth. Wow. It's absolutely wrecking regular cheese steaks for me right now. ... Dynamite, dynamite. I mean, this isn't just like, hey, my grandmother was Polish. This is like a legitimate, groundbreaking destination dish.

restaurants.name: Sidecar
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in a great Triple D town, Philadelphia, in southwest Center City, to check out a joint that for 60 years was just a shot and beer place. Now it's three stories of upscale bar food. This is Sidecar.

dishes.description: Potato gnocchi made with riced potatoes, seasoned and mixed with eggs, chives, and flour. Served in a clam chowder base with applewood smoked bacon, shallots, garlic, little neck clams, clam stock, cream, and butter. Finished with crispy shallots and a celery leaf salad.
dishes.guy_response: The gnocchi's delicate. Depth and flavor. ... It's the creaminess in the gnocchi itself. Mmm. And then the salad on it. The salad on the top is what makes it wow. This is literally one of the best gnocchi dishes I've ever had. Oh, awesome, man.

restaurants.name: Stogie Joe's
restaurants.guy_intro: They're serving up their mom's recipes in an old neighborhood bar. This is Stogie Joe's.

dishes.description: Crab gravy made with olive oil, butter, leeks, onion, celery, garlic, spices, blue crabs, Old Bay, white wine, and crushed plum tomatoes, cooked for hours and then macerated through a food mill. Served with linguine.
dishes.guy_response: It is crab on crab on crab. You're getting all the flavor you want. Wham! Right into the pasta dish. It's flavor city, really delicious.

restaurants.name: The Gall & Company Malt House
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here to check out a joint where two real estate buddies came upon a great deal on a pub and they said, hey, why don't we get into the pub and restaurant business? You're going to dig it. This is the Gall & Company Malt House.

dishes.description: A take on a cheesesteak featuring housemade kielbasa (ground pork, paprika, onion powder, black pepper, salt, garlic) baked into a loaf, sliced, and grilled. Served on a roll with house-cut fries, caramelized onions, and a sharp American cheese sauce.
dishes.guy_response: I don't think I've ever had anything like it. It is part ham and cheese. It is part cheesesteak. There's those big flavors that you get in kielbasa. And the uniqueness of how you do it from making it into the loaf to slicing it to throwing it on the flat top, kind of caramelizing the sausage. That is a destination sandwich.

restaurants.name: Hardina
restaurants.guy_intro: I've heard about this funky little Indonesian joint that is run by two daughters and their mom. This is Hardina.

dishes.dish_name: Tempeh Fritters with Spicy Pickled Veggies (Achar)
dishes.description: Housemade tempeh, fermented soybeans, cut and dipped in a batter with garlic powder, salt, pepper, egg, water, and scallions, then deep-fried. Served with achar, a side of spicy pickled vegetables including cabbage, cucumber, red peppers, carrots, and pineapple.
dishes.guy_response: Dynamite. I'm telling you, the first time you ever tried tempeh, you should come here and try it. It's got delicious texture. It's a bit nutty. I love the batter with the green onions. You get that little kick. Fantastic.
